summary: Build `parrot devloop` — a Rich/prompt_toolkit interactive console registered in the existing LazyGroup CLI — that collects a WorkBrief via pydantic-driven prompts, dispatches a dev-loop run, renders the live event/envelope stream, and answers HITL gates interactively. Every hard primitive already exists: DevLoopRunner is a complete programmatic command surface (run/resolve_gate/cancel/registry), WorkBrief carries field descriptions usable to auto-generate prompts, the FEAT-322 session-state layer provides sequenced envelopes + snapshot rebuild for both in-process and remote consumption, and the `parrot agent` REPL (console-cli-agents) provides the exact CLI architecture to mirror (Click entry, Rich renderer, slash commands, standalone-vs-server loaders).

localization.role: programmatic surface the CLI drives: run(), run_revision(), resolve_gate(), cancel_run(), get_host(), registry_state()
the pydantic structured input to collect interactively (kind, summary, description, existing_issue_key = Jira ticket, acceptance_criteria, ...)
current WS-only read surface (Redis stream fan-in, view=state snapshot) — what a server-mode CLI consumes
REST write surface (resolve gate / cancel) — what a server-mode CLI calls
registration point: add 'devloop': 'parrot.cli.devloop' (installed via existing [project.scripts] parrot = parrot.cli:cli)
architecture template: Click entry + Rich renderer + SlashCommandDispatcher + standalone/server dual-mode loaders
precedent for Rich-rendered interactive HITL answering from a terminal
the only current UI host (aiohttp + vanilla-JS client) — confirms the 'websocket-UI-only' pain in the source

constraints.claim: rich>=13.0, click>=8.1.7, prompt_toolkit>=3.0 are already core dependencies; textual is NOT — a full-screen TUI would add a new dependency
models.py (WorkBrief) is import-cheap (zero internal deps beyond pydantic), so the wizard can validate without booting the flow stack
Embedded (in-process) mode requires the real-mode prerequisites: Redis, claude CLI, Jira credentials (per examples/dev_loop/README.md); server mode only needs HTTP/WS reachability
dev_loop internals (dispatcher) are hot/actively churning (FEAT-322/323 in last 4 months); the CLI should bind only to DevLoopRunner + envelope/REST surfaces
HITL gates have TTLs, arbitration and 409-on-double-resolve; reconnect state rebuild exists via multiplexer view=state

hypotheses.statement: Implement `parrot devloop` as a new `parrot.cli.devloop` module mirroring the `parrot agent` REPL architecture: a WorkBrief wizard (pydantic-driven prompts incl. Jira key and proposal/brainstorm path), a run view that renders SessionHost envelopes with Rich Live, interactive gate resolution, and dual attach modes (embedded DevLoopRunner in-process; remote server via WS multiplexer + REST commands).
hypotheses.reasoning: Every building block exists and is stable; the feature is a composition + one new rendering loop, not new flow machinery.

confidence_map.claim: Registration is one dict entry in cli._lazy_commands + one module; command installs as `parrot devloop` via existing [project.scripts]
WorkBrief.model_fields carries descriptions/defaults/Literal choices sufficient to auto-generate an interactive wizard
DevLoopRunner.run/resolve_gate/cancel_run is sufficient for full interactive control in embedded mode
Server-mode reads via WS multiplexer (view=state) + writes via REST commands cover remote attach, including reconnect
The 'description or path of proposal/brainstorm' input maps onto WorkBrief.description (file contents inlined) — no model change needed
A run-start REST endpooint for server mode may not exist yet outside examples/dev_loop/server.py (only gate/cancel routes found in core)
Embedded mode works from any checkout with Redis + claude CLI + Jira creds

unknowns.question: Which attach mode should v1 ship: embedded in-process runner, remote client to a running ai-parrot server, or both (like the agent REPL's Standalone/ServerProxy loaders)?
unknowns.user_answer: Embedded only — in-process DevLoopRunner; no remote server client in v1.

unknowns.question: Should the WorkBrief wizard be a generic reusable pydantic-model→interactive-prompt engine (usable by other CLIs), or a hand-written wizard specific to WorkBrief?
unknowns.user_answer: Generic engine — reusable pydantic-model→interactive-prompt engine, instantiated for WorkBrief/RevisionBrief.

unknowns.question: Is v1 scope 'start new runs only', or also list/attach to existing runs (registry_state) and revision-mode runs (RevisionBrief)?
unknowns.user_answer: Everything incl. revision — new runs, list/attach, and RevisionBrief revision-mode runs in v1.

unknowns.question: Console style: Claude-Code-like scrolling Rich Live console (no new deps) vs full-screen TUI (would add textual as a dependency)?
unknowns.user_answer: Rich Live scrolling console (Claude-Code-like); no textual dependency.

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence and single-hypothesis; the remaining unknowns are product-scope decisions resolvable in Q&A, after which the feature is spec-ready (likely 5-7 tasks).
